art_style: Watercolor and ink wash blending photorealistic Vietnamese urban landscapes with surreal anatomical distortions, where human figures dissolve into rain and architectural details bleed like wet ink on textured paper.
color_palette: indigo downpour, concrete grey, oxidized bronze, faded lotus pink, warm sepia, muted teal, bone white
texture: Wet paper grain with deliberate water damage, resembling rain-soaked notebook pages left on a café table
mood: Melancholic permanence — the visual weight of things slowly dissolving while remaining achingly present
reference_artists: Phạm Huy Thông, Dinh Q. Lê, Trần Lương
cover_concept: Six empty chairs at a wet café table seen through a rain-streaked window, with one chair's silhouette slightly more faded than the others, and ghostly water reflections on the glass showing figures that don't match the empty scene
ornament_concept: Small rain droplets containing fragmented silhouettes — half-formed faces, partial hands reaching, the outline of a chair — rendered in single-line ink strokes that trail off like unfinished memories
scene_break_concept: A thin horizontal line of three unevenly spaced raindrops, the middle one slightly larger, bleeding faintly downward
map_concept: A hand-drawn map of Huế where streets and landmarks shift slightly between faded and sharp focus, with the Perfume River rendered as a visible vein pulsing through the city, and constant rain indicated by vertical strokes that obscure the edges into white nothing
